zh.lifeSummary: 當James Hill於1493年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲時，他的父親James Hill23歲，母親Cicely Riddlesworth23歲。他於1516年與Margaret Ware在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1545年6月10日在他的家鄉享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Hill于1493年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲时，他的父亲James Hill23岁，母亲Cicely Riddlesworth23岁。他于1516年与Margaret Ware在英国英格兰素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1545年6月10日在他的家乡享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Hill was born in 1493, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, James Hill, was 23 and his mother, Cicely Riddlesworth, was 23. He married Margaret Ware in 1516, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 June 1545, in his hometown, at the age of 52.

Il a épousé Margaret Ware en 1516, à Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 juin 1545, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: James Hill(이)가 1493년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Hill(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Cicely Riddlesworth(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1516년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 Margaret Ware(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1545년 6월 10일 그의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Hill родился в 1493 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени James Hill было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Cicely Riddlesworth, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Ware в 1516 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 июня 1545 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 52 лет.
